sections.heading: These Regulations are the Cybersecurity (Provider-Owned Critical Information
sections.summary: These Regulations define the Cybersecurity (Provider-Owned Critical Information Infrastructure) Regulations 2018 and their effective date.
sections.source_quote: These Regulations are the Cybersecurity (Provider-Owned Critical Information Infrastructure) Regulations 2018 and come into operation on 31 August 2018.

sections.heading: In these Regulations, unless the context otherwise requires —
sections.summary: Defines terms related to provider-owned critical information infrastructure, including owner-controlled and supplier-controlled systems, and working days.
sections.dtia_summary: Defines critical infrastructure terms for DTIA compliance; specifies system types and working days for reporting.

requirements.text: owner-controlled interconnected computer or computer system
owner-controlled non-interconnected computer or computer system
supplier-controlled interconnected computer or computer system

sections.source_quote: In these Regulations, unless the context otherwise requires — "owner-controlled interconnected computer or computer system" means any computer or computer system under the control of the owner of a provider-owned critical information infrastructure, that is interconnected with or that communicates with the provider-owned critical information infrastructure;
